ui.title: ExifSense | Forensic Intelligence
ui.hero_desc: You don't need to be an expert to understand the hidden information in your photos. ExifSense makes it easy for anyone to read location, device, and image history—securely and privately.
ui.removal_modal_desc: Select which information you would like to strip from this image.
ui.dropzone_prompt: Drop your images here or click to browse
ui.advisory_mobile_warn: Mobile Users: Capturing photos directly via browser may result in missing geospatial metadata due to browser privacy sandboxing.
ui.advisory_recommend: To preserve maximum forensic intelligence, we recommend using your device's native camera app first, then selecting the file from your gallery via the "Files" option.
ui.feature_secure_desc: 100% local processing. Your sensitive evidence assets never leave your browser, ensuring absolute data privacy and chain of custody.
ui.feature_expert_desc: Automated narratives that explain technical data in plain language. Transform raw metadata into meaningful information.
ui.feature_report_desc: Export cross-asset findings into industry-standard PDF, Markdown, or CSV. Professional reports ready for documentation.
ui.confirm_message: Are you sure you want to proceed?
ui.history_empty: Your history is empty. Start a new analysis to see items here.
ui.no_exif_desc: This image has been stripped of its metadata or did not contain any to begin with.
ui.no_gps: No GPS signatures found in this asset.
ui.history_delete_confirm_msg: Are you sure you want to permanently purge the record for "{name}"? This action cannot be reversed.
ui.history_clear_confirm_msg: This will permanently remove all forensic sessions from your browser storage. This action cannot be reversed.
ui.reupload_required_msg: Metadata removal requires the original file data. Since you are accessing this from history, please re-upload the original file in the New Analysis tab using the "Remove Metadata" mode.
ui.sanitization_complete_msg: Your image has been cleaned and the download has started.
ui.err_batch_limit: Batch limit exceeded. Please upload a maximum of {n} files at once.
ui.err_invalid_files: Please select valid image files (max 100MB each).
ui.err_sanitize_failed: Forensic sanitization failed. Please try again.

narratives.hardware_software: The device was running {software} at the time of capture.
narratives.hardware_analysis: Analysis indicates a standard hardware profile for this device class.
narratives.hardware_mod: This asset was generated using {make} {model}{lens}. Note: Processing artifacts from {software} were detected, indicating potential forensic alteration or post-production.
narratives.exposure: Captured at {aperture} with a shutter speed of {shutter} at {iso}{program}.
narratives.exp_low_light: The high ISO sensitivity suggest this asset was captured in a low-light or indoor environment.
narratives.exp_bright: The low ISO and fast exposure indicate a well-lit environment, likely outdoors during daylight.
narratives.exp_shallow_depth: The wide aperture suggests a shallow depth of field, likely used to isolate the subject from the background.
narratives.exp_standard: The exposure parameters suggest a balanced lighting environment with standard automated compensation.
narratives.optics: The lens was set to a focal length of {focal}{focal35}{zoom}. This configuration suggests a specific perspective alignment for the captured subject.
narratives.quality: Image resolution is {width}x{height} pixels. Processing analysis indicates a {contrast} contrast profile and {sharpness} sharpness level within the {colorSpace} color space.
narratives.timeline_mod_note: Note: Modification detected ({modStr}). This suggests potential post-processing or metadata alteration.
narratives.timeline_integrity: Chronological integrity appears consistent across available timestamps.
narratives.no_chrono: No chronological data could be definitively established from the metadata.
narratives.geospatial: Digital location analysis places the device at {locationName}. The coordinates correspond to {lat}, {lng}. No GPS spoofing signatures detected in the coordinate precision pattern.
narratives.no_geo: No coordinate signatures established. This asset does not contain valid GPS metadata for geospatial reconstruction.
narratives.combined_hw_consistent: All assets in this session were captured using the same hardware profile: {device}. This suggests a unified source or a single operator.
narratives.combined_hw_discrepancy: Multiple devices detected: {deviceList}. This cross-device capture pattern is common in collaborative efforts or distributed evidence gathering.
narratives.combined_timeline: Evidence spans a timeline of {diffHours} hours. The sequence begins at {start} and concludes at {end}. No chronological overlaps or timestamp manipulation signatures detected.
narratives.combined_geo_concentrated: Assets form a highly concentrated cluster within a 50m radius. This indicates a stationary capture session at a single tactical position.
narratives.combined_geo_localized: Geospatial spread shows a localized operation within a {dist}m range. The movement pattern is consistent with a pedestrian-scale investigation.
narratives.combined_geo_trail: Analysis established a geographic trail spanning {dist} km. The spatial distribution suggests a mobile capture environment or multiple transit points.
narratives.velocity_warning: Warning: Impossible velocity detected ({speed} km/h). Potential metadata spoofing.
narratives.sanitization_alert: Sanitization Signature Detected: This file lacks standard hardware metadata, which frequently indicates processing via messaging apps (e.g., WhatsApp, Telegram) or metadata stripping tools.
narratives.null_island_warn: Location Anomaly: Coordinates point to 'Null Island' (0,0). This suggests a corrupted or programmatically zeroed sensor payload.
narratives.precision_warn: Precision Anomaly: Coordinates exhibit integer alignment. Natural GPS paths rarely map to flat decimal structures, implying potential manual spoofing.
narratives.combined_serial_match: Physical Provenance Verified: All assets in this batch match the identical hardware serial number {serial}.
